root_text.an7.19:1.1: “Nibbāne sukhānupassī viharati sukhasaññī sukhapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.19:1.2: So āsavānaṁ khayā …pe… sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an7.19:1.3: Ayaṁ bhikkhave, paṭhamo puggalo āhuneyyo …pe… puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.19:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo nibbāne sukhānupassī viharati sukhasaññī sukhapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.19:2.2: Tassa apubbaṁ acarimaṁ āsavapariyādānañca hoti jīvitapariyādānañca.
root_text.an7.19:2.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo puggalo āhuneyyo …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.19:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo nibbāne sukhānupassī viharati sukhasaññī sukhapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.19:3.2: So pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti …pe…
root_text.an7.19:3.7: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, sattamo puggalo āhuneyyo …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.19:3.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, satta puggalā āhuneyyā …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.

translation_text.an7.19:1.1: “One meditates observing the happiness in extinguishment. They perceive happiness and experience happiness. Constantly, continually, and without interruption, they apply the mind and fathom with wisdom.
translation_text.an7.19:1.2: They’ve realized the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an7.19:1.3: This is the first individual worthy of offerings.
translation_text.an7.19:2.1: Next, take an individual who meditates observing the happiness in extinguishment. They perceive happiness and experience happiness. Constantly, continually, and without interruption, they apply the mind and fathom with wisdom.
translation_text.an7.19:2.2: Their defilements and their life come to an end at exactly the same time.
translation_text.an7.19:3.1: Next, take an individual who meditates observing the happiness in extinguishment. They perceive happiness and experience happiness. Constantly, continually, and without interruption, they apply the mind and fathom with wisdom.
translation_text.an7.19:3.2: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished between one life and the next. …
translation_text.an7.19:3.3: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished upon landing. …
translation_text.an7.19:3.4: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished without extra effort. …
translation_text.an7.19:3.5: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished with extra effort. …
translation_text.an7.19:3.6: With the ending of the five lower fetters they head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm.
translation_text.an7.19:3.8: These are the seven individuals who are worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and are the supreme field of merit for the world.”
